title: Agent Recall
description: Made past agent work searchable again, with results that point back to the original sessions instead of becoming loose summaries.
seoTitle: Agent Recall Case Study | Local AI Agent Memory Infrastructure
seoDescription: A case study on Agent Recall, an open-source Rust CLI for local transcript search, SQLite FTS5 indexing, memory extraction, and receipt-backed AI agent context retrieval.
constraints: Agent transcripts can contain sensitive project context, so raw archives needed to remain local and stay the source of truth.
Search results and memory records had to point back to source files and line numbers instead of becoming unverifiable summaries.
problem: Useful decisions, blockers, commands, and implementation details were scattered across local agent session archives.
Plain text search over raw JSONL was too noisy. Treating transcripts as the database would also make recall brittle and hard to audit.
solution: Built a Rust CLI that streams supported transcript archives, redacts common secret patterns, parses high-signal events, and indexes them into a disposable SQLite FTS5 database.
Added search, recent, day, show, bundle, pins, memories, related context, delta cursors, MCP-style resources, doctor, stats, and JSON output for agent-facing workflows.
Added a watcher with macOS LaunchAgent support, freshness state, repo/date filters, lock handling, and an optional forensic importer for archived Codex SQLite logs.
result: Shipped a public, installable tool for local recall across Codex, OMP, Pi, Claude, and optional archived Codex log sources.
Made retrieval auditable: search results group by session and include source labels, event kinds, file paths, and exact line receipts.
Covered the risky paths with CLI, ranking, redaction, parser, memory extraction, watcher, and LaunchAgent tests so the tool can change without quietly breaking recall quality.
